prompt:
Analytics infra ticket. We export catalog snapshots out of Snowflake into our reconciliation pipeline, and the current exporter goes through the database/sql driver, which hides partition boundaries and quietly reformats values — we've been burned twice by lossy round-trips of NUMBER(12,2) strings. Snowflake's SQL API v2 hands results over as string-encoded partitions, which is exactly what a faithful exporter wants. Build me a small Go package `sfresults` (module already set up as go-snowflake-partition-results, package at the module root, stdlib only — no driver, no third-party modules). Split it into sensible files: client/transport, result decoding, errors; I'd expect 3+ source files.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Snowflake SQL API docs on docs.snowflake.com — at least the SQL API reference and the handling-responses page. Confirm from the vendor's own pages how partitioned results actually work: where partition 0's data lives, how additional partitions are fetched, what partitionInfo/rowType contain, and which headers every request needs. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json, provenance docs/official_sources.json — protected fixtures, as is sfresults_test.go. If live docs disagree with a pin, call it out in your summary and leave fixtures/tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Surface (see the test file for exact types):
- NewClient(Config{BaseURL, Token, TokenType, UserAgent, HTTPClient, NewRequestID}) — every request carries the bearer token, the token-type header, Accept, and OUR User-Agent (never Go's default). NewRequestID supplies the requestId query value on submits.
- ReadAll(ctx, Statement{SQL, Timeout, Database, Schema, Warehouse, Role}) submits the statement and returns the complete ResultSet: StatementHandle, NumRows, Columns (name/type/precision/scale/nullable from rowType), Partitions (rowCount/uncompressedSize/compressedSize), and Rows with every value kept as the exact string Snowflake sent — Value{Valid,Raw}, SQL NULL as Valid=false. Unset optional statement fields stay out of the JSON body entirely.
- Partition discipline: the submit response's inline data is partition 0 — never re-fetch it; walk the remaining partitions strictly in ascending order, exactly once each, and validate each batch's row count against partitionInfo (and the grand total against numRows) — a mismatch is an error that names the partition.
- Failure discipline: a 422 QueryFailureStatus becomes APIError{Status,Code,SQLState,Message,StatementHandle}; a 404 mid-read means the handle expired — return HandleExpiredError{StatementHandle,Partition} and stop immediately, no retries, no further fetches. The token never appears in any error text.

Acceptance: `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` against the loopback fake in sfresults_test.go (dummy creds only). Don't touch the test file or docs/.

research.interface: Snowflake SQL API v2, POST /api/v2/statements + GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}?partition=N (GA; jsonv2 result format)

official_sources.title: Snowflake SQL API reference
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/v2/statements and GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}; the partition query parameter; ResultSet schema with resultSetMetaData (numRows, format, rowType with name/type/length/precision/scale/nullable, partitionInfo with rowCount/uncompressedSize/compressedSize); QueryFailureStatus schema; status codes 200/404/422/429

official_sources.title: Handling responses
official_sources.used_for: results split into partitions sized by the server; the first response carries partition 0 inline in data; additional partitions retrieved with GET ?partition=N; rows encoded as arrays of strings with JSON null for SQL NULL; partition responses carry data only, metadata is not repeated

official_sources.title: Submitting a request to execute SQL statements
official_sources.used_for: required Authorization/Accept/Content-Type/User-Agent headers, requestId UUID query parameter, request body fields statement/timeout/database/schema/warehouse/role and their case-sensitivity

research.language_rationale: Snowflake's Go driver targets database/sql for interactive use; for a batch exporter that must see partition boundaries, raw sizes and string-exact values, the documented SQL API v2 REST surface is the intended driverless integration path, and Go's net/http + encoding/json stdlib is the idiomatic zero-dependency client.
